solution.p: We find a number that divides into both the numerator 14, and the denominator 27.

Check the first few prime numbers one by one: 2, 3, 5, 7, \ldots, until the prime number is greater than or equal to 14. No prime number goes into both 14 and 27, so the fraction {{\frac{14}{27}}} is not reducible.
A second method to reduce fraction is to prime factor both the numerator and the denominator, and then cancel out factors in pairs: one from the numerator and one from the denominator.
Notice that no numbers can be canceled out, so the fraction {{\frac{14}{27}}} is not reducible.
